purpose: Defines weapon/class pose template anchor requirements before generating new body, weapon, armor, and class test sprites.

coordinateConvention.sideRule: ScreenLeft and ScreenRight are screen-space labels, not anatomical labels.

rules: Do not derive runtime attachment points from finished weapon/class test image bounding boxes.
Each weapon/class pose must expose explicit grip, body, and hit/effect anchors before class sprites are generated.
Pose-specific anchors extend the base body anchor set; they do not replace Head_Top, Neck_Base, Chest_Center, Hip_Center, hands, and foot baselines.
Feet stay locked for standing idle/guard templates unless the template explicitly declares walk, dash, jump, or knockback foot motion.
Equipment, armor, cloak, and held items follow anchors and motion frames rather than independent CSS-only movement.

poseTemplates.notes: Baseline weapon-and-shield stance. It may reuse idle_default until arms are redrawn.
Two-handed weapons need both hand grips before the weapon image can be judged.
Bow draw must expose arrow anchors separately from the bow image bounds.
Staff pose keeps a vertical weapon axis and a separate cast hand for skill VFX.
Wand-book pose keeps the cast hand, wand tip, and supported grimoire anchors separate for runtime VFX and offhand attachment checks.
Gauntlet and knuckle classes need fist centers in addition to the normal hand grips.

implementationOrder: Lock this pose-template matrix before producing representative weapon/class test sprites.
Draw or generate source bodies for each template using the required anchors.
Create pixelized versions only after source anchors are approved.
Attach equipment proxies to each template and compare frame reports before approving final class tests.
